description: Colombian narcoparamilitary group formed by dissidents of the AUC after its demobilization in 2006. It became independent following the murder of its founder in Venezuela in 2008 and was one of Colombia's most important drug trafficking organizations. The group finances itself through cocaine, marijuana, and heroin trafficking, as well as illegal gold mining. It operated mainly in Valle del Cauca and Cali, with influence extending to other parts of Colombia and western Venezuela. By 2017, the group had fragmented into smaller factions.

country_links.evidence_quote: Javier Antonio Calle Serna (alias "Comba"), se entregó a las autoridades estadounidenses en Aruba
country_links.context: One of Los Rastrojos' leaders surrendered to US authorities in Aruba.

country_links.evidence_quote: En 2019 se presume su reaparición en la frontera con ... Ecuador
country_links.context: The organization operates in the border region with Ecuador.

country_links.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos mueven drogas principalmente por la costa del Pacífico hasta Centroamérica y México, donde las venden a narcotraficantes mexicanos
country_links.context: Los Rastrojos sell drugs to Mexican traffickers in Mexico.
Los Rastrojos export cocaine and heroin to the United States via routes through Mexico and Venezuela.

country_links.evidence_quote: hay informes de que han extendido su zona de influencia a otras partes de Colombia y al oeste de Venezuela
country_links.context: Los Rastrojos have extended their influence into western Venezuela, control smuggling routes into Venezuela, and have members killed or arrested there.
